flashcards.front: What is the PDF of the Exponential Distribution?
flashcards.back: f(x) = λe^(-λx) for x ≥ 0, where λ > 0 is the rate parameter. f(x) = 0 for x < 0.

flashcards.front: What are the mean and variance of an Exponential Distribution with rate parameter λ?
flashcards.back: Mean E(X) = 1/λ. Variance Var(X) = 1/λ². Note that the standard deviation equals the mean: σ = 1/λ.

flashcards.front: State the memoryless property of the Exponential Distribution.
flashcards.back: P(X > s + t | X > s) = P(X > t) for all s, t ≥ 0. This means the probability of surviving an additional time t is independent of how long it has already survived.

flashcards.front: What is the CDF of the Exponential Distribution?
flashcards.back: F(x) = 1 - e^(-λx) for x ≥ 0. The survival function is P(X > x) = e^(-λx).

flashcards.front: How is the Exponential Distribution related to the Poisson Distribution?
flashcards.back: If events follow a Poisson process with rate λ, then the time between consecutive events (inter-arrival time) follows Exp(λ). Poisson counts events; Exponential measures waiting time.

flashcards.front: What is the Moment Generating Function (MGF) of the Exponential Distribution?
flashcards.back: M_X(t) = λ / (λ - t) for t < λ.
